content.problemStatement:
Chef calls a sequence of integers A1,A2,‚Ä¶,ANA_1, A_2, \ldots, A_NA1‚Äã,A2‚Äã,‚Ä¶,AN‚Äã good if it satisfies the following conditions:


Ai‚äïAi+1>0A_i \oplus A_{i+1} \gt 0Ai‚Äã‚äïAi+1‚Äã>0 for all valid iii
Ai‚àí1‚äïAi‚äïAi+1=AiA_{i-1} \oplus A_i \oplus A_{i+1} = A_iAi‚àí1‚Äã‚äïAi‚Äã‚äïAi+1‚Äã=Ai‚Äã for all valid iii
In particular, any sequence with length 111 is good and any sequence of length 222 which satisfies the first condition is good.

Here, ‚äï\oplus‚äï denotes the bitwise XOR operation.

Chef gives you a sequence A1,A2,‚Ä¶,ANA_1, A_2, \ldots, A_NA1‚Äã,A2‚Äã,‚Ä¶,AN‚Äã. You may perform the following operation on the sequence any number of times (possibly 000): choose 333 pairwise distinct valid indices aaa, bbb and ccc and change AcA_cAc‚Äã to Aa‚äïAbA_a \oplus A_bAa‚Äã‚äïAb‚Äã. Note that this means the operation can only be performed if N‚â•3N \geq 3N‚â•3.

Chef is asking you to make the sequence good using at most 3‚ãÖN3 \cdot N3‚ãÖN operations or report that it is impossible. **Note that you do not have to minimise the number of performed operations.**
constraints: the sum of NNN over all test cases does not exceed 2‚ãÖ1042 \cdot 10^42‚ãÖ104
`Explanation**Example case 1:** The sequence is already good, so performing 000 operations is a valid solution.
**Example case 2:** The sequence cannot be made good using at most 3‚ãÖN3 \cdot N3‚ãÖN operations.

**Example case 3:** We can make the sequence good by performing the 444 operations shown on the output, in this order. Note that the initial sequence is also good, so performing 000 operations is also a valid solution.
**Example case 4:** The sequence does not satisfy the first condition and since N=2N=2N=2, we cannot perform any operations on it.
content.inputFormat:
The first line of the input contains a single integer TTT denoting the number of test cases. The description of TTT test cases follows.
The first line of each test case contains a single integer NNN.
The second line contains NNN space-separated integers A1,A2,‚Ä¶,ANA_1, A_2, \ldots, A_NA1‚Äã,A2‚Äã,‚Ä¶,AN‚Äã.
content.outputFormat:
For each test case:


If it is impossible to change the given sequence into a good sequence using at most 3‚ãÖN3 \cdot N3‚ãÖN operations, print a single line containing the integer ‚àí1-1‚àí1.
Otherwise, first, print a line containing a single integer MMM (0‚â§M‚â§3‚ãÖN0 \leq M \leq 3 \cdot N0‚â§M‚â§3‚ãÖN) ‚Äï the number of operations you want to perform.
Then, print MMM lines describing these operations in the order in which you want to perform them. For each iii (1‚â§i‚â§M1 \leq i \leq M1‚â§i‚â§M), the iii-th of these lines should contain three space-separated integers aia_iai‚Äã, bib_ibi‚Äã and cic_ici‚Äã (pairwise distinct; 1‚â§ai,bi,ci‚â§N1 \leq a_i, b_i, c_i \leq N1‚â§ai‚Äã,bi‚Äã,ci‚Äã‚â§N) ‚Äï the indices on which the iii-th operation is performed.
If there are multiple solutions, you may find any one of them.
